programmes.show_name: The Case of the Frightened Lady
programmes.show_description: 1983. Drama. Director: Christopher Menaul. Stars Virginia McKenna, Warren Clarke & Elizabeth Garvie. Who murdered the young chauffeur? Detective Chief Superintendent Tanner leads the investigation.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life For Crying Out
programmes.show_description: ... Loud. 1964. A fascinating survey of the advertising industry, with a focus on how it works and what it does.
1963. Who Killed Alex Debbs? Directed by Don Weis. Stars Gene Barry, Gary Conway & Regis Toomey. The publisher of a girlie magazine is murdered at one of his own key clubs.
1965. Take Me In Paris. Gig Young, David Niven and Charles Boyer are The Rogues, members of two related families of international forgers and conmen.
The Alchemist. 1955. Stars Richard Greene, Victor Woolf & Rufus Cruikshank. The Merry Men & Robin Hood protect England from the evil Prince John while King Richard is fighting in the Crusades.
The Hand of the Hawk. 1956. Stars Robert Shaw, Anthony Dawson & Sid James. Tempest tries to solve the murder of a crew member from another ship. A mysterious clue may just hold the answer.
The Magic Powder. 1958. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. Doctor Klein, a scientist working for Gessler, invents an explosive powder for use in roadmaking.
Founder of Talking Pictures TV and one half of the Footage Detectives, Noel Cronin, shares shows and films not to be missed throughout April.
1941. Musical starring James Stewart, Paulette Goddard and Horace Heidt. Jimmy, the owner of a failed music shop, goes to work with his uncle, the owner of a food factory.
1949. Mystery. Directed by Rudolph Maté. Stars Edmond O'Brien, Pamela Britton & Luther Adler. An accountant learns that he has been poisoned, with only a few days to live, he hunts down his enemy.
1940. War. Director: Terry Morse. Stars Boris Karloff, Margaret Lindsay, Bruce Lester & Leonard Mudie. Bennett's home is a hotbed for spies. But no one knows the identity of the German spymaster.
1967. Western. Directed by Robert D. Webb. Stars Vincent Price, Diana Ivarson & Robert Gunner. In South Africa, a wily old gold miner and his granddaughter are besieged by a group of bank robbers.
The Wolf of Banbury. 1962. Stars Dermot Walsh, Frances De Wolff, John Bennett. Richard learns that Baron Giles is giving protection to fugitives from justice and that he has captured Lady Rosalie.
1963. Comedy. Directed by John & Roy Boulting. Stars Peter Sellers, Ian Carmichael, Cecil Parker & Eric Sykes. Due to mistaken identity, a minister is accidently appointed to a snobbish parish.
1952. Mystery. Directed by Sam Newfield. Stars Cesar Romero, Lois Maxwell & Bernadette O'Farrell. A woman enlists the help of an American journalist to solve the mystery of her brother's death.
1944. War Drama. Director: Charles Crichton. Stars David Farrar, Ralph Michael & Robert Wyndham. A WWII flyer fails to join the RAF so he joins the Air - Sea Rescue instead.
Stranglehold. 1955. Stars John Bentley, Christopher Lee & Maureen Swanson. Peter Stanhope's ex-wife turns up after being presumed dead in the war. Betty and the butler plan a nasty blackmail.
The Spinners of The Spools bring a real treat for Milkmen, a fabulous reel on JOBS dairy from the 60s! Then tips from 1951, a brilliant reel on the ABC Film Review magazine.

programmes.show_name: Francis Durbridge's A Game of Murder
programmes.show_description: 1966. Stars Gerald Harper, June Barry & Conrad Phillips. More secrets are uncovered by Jack Kerry. Are the pieces of this mystery slowly coming together?
... Blackmailer. 1992. Director: Peter Hammond. Stars Jeremy Brett, Edward Hardwicke & Robert Hardy. Holmes and Watson attempt to break the grip of a ruthless blackmailer of their clients.
1958. Sci fi. Directed by Irvin S. Yeaworth Jr. Stars Steve McQueen, Aneta Corsaut & Earl Rowe. Teenagers Steve and Jane attempt to save their town from a gelatinous creature from another planet.
King for A Day. Stars: Adam Faith, Iain Cuthbertson & Lynn Dalby. It seems that Budgie has hit the big time when Charlie Endell clothes him in a suit and makes him chief associate.
1945. Drama. Director: Fritz Lang. Stars Edward G. Robinson, Joan Bennett & Dan Duryea. A banker in a dying marriage falls for a thug's girlfriend. They find a way to cash in on his infatuation.
1952. Starring Mark Stevens & Angela Lansbury. During the war of 1812, the U.S. arranges to borrow gold from France. A young Captain must navigate through the British blockade.
1965. Rockabye The Hard Way. Classic crime series produced by Aaron Spelling and starring Anne Francis. A suave master thief starts dating Honey in the hopes of getting inside information.
Natchez Trace. 1956. Stars Scott Forbes, Will Wright & Brad Morrow. A group of travellers on their way to Natchez ask Jim to protect them against a bandit who preys on people headed there.
1950. Comedy. Directed by Mario Soldati. Stars Jean Kent, Robert Beatty & Margaret Rutherford. When a gangster learns that a bank clerk is his exact double, he takes a chance at robbing the bank.
1935. Mystery. Directed by Leslie S. Hiscott. Stars Arthur Wontner, Lyn Harding & Jane Carr. Sherlock Holmes puts his retirement on hold to investigate one last case with his sidekick Dr. Watson.
1957. Thriller. Director: Gerald Thomas. Stars John Mills, Wilfrid Hyde-White & Lionel Jeffries. Howard finds himself in a predicament after he finds an actress murdered in his flat.
1949. Richard Todd, Ronald Reagan & Patricia Neal star in this sensitive adaptation of John Patrick's play about a soldier with little time left & the friendships he makes with those around him.
The Gauntlet of St Gerhardt. 1958. Stars Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. A religious relic, the Gauntlet of St Gerhardt, appears to inspire the Swiss to feats of valour.
1949. Adventure. Director: Henri Decoin. Stars George Marchal, Suzanne Dehelly & Pierre Fresnay. Carbot, the head of a French aviation company, decides to expand his business into America.
1954. Thriller. Director: Lewis Gilbert. Stars Robert Morley, Joan Collins, Laurence Harvey & Stanley Baker. A group of down-and-outs are holding up a post office van but things start to go wrong.
Take a step back in time and enjoy the hit 60s tune Runaround Sue by Dion.
The Actress. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Jennifer Jayne & Lew Luton. An attractive actress is kidnapped by Mike Upton. Cobb tries to rescue her until they run into some angry gold miners.
The Deadly Decoy Affair. 1964. Stars Robert Vaughn, David McCallum & Leo G. Carroll. Solo and Kuryakin are told to escort a captured Thrush official to Washington.

programmes.show_name: The Mind of Mr. J. G. Reeder
programmes.show_description: 1969. The Stealer of Marble. Stars: Hugh Burden, Willoughby Goddard & Mona Bruce. The case of the disappearance of Billingham, Director of Telfers Trust, who has absconded with £150,000.
Murders in Avignon - Meurtres à Avignon. 2015. Director: Stéphane Kappes. Stars Catherine Jacob & Laëtitia Milot. Police investigate a skeleton found in a secret chamber in the Palais des Papes.
Caroline Munro returns with another brilliant episode delving into the World of Hammer. Tonight, we look at Wicked Women including screen legends Bette Davis, Joan Fontaine and Martine Beswick.

programmes.show_name: Hammer House of Mystery and Suspense
programmes.show_description: In Possession. 1984. Stars Carol Lynley, Christopher Cazenove, David Healy. A couple becomes trapped in a replay of a sinister event that happened in their apartment in the past.
